meta.book: Lay, Linear Algebra and Its Applications

preambles.text: The following statements refer to vectors in R^n (or R^m) with the standard inner product.

items.label: 1a
items.text: The length of every vector is a positive number.

items.label: 1b
items.text: A vector v and its negative −v have equal lengths.

items.label: 1c
items.text: The distance between u and v is ‖u − v‖.

items.label: 1d
items.text: If r is any scalar, then ‖rv‖ = r‖v‖.

items.label: 1e
items.text: If two vectors are orthogonal, they are linearly independent.

items.label: 1f
items.text: If x is orthogonal to both u and v, then x must be orthogonal to u − v.

items.label: 1g
items.text: If ‖u + v‖^2 = ‖u‖^2 + ‖v‖^2, then u and v are orthogonal.

items.label: 1h
items.text: If ‖u − v‖^2 = ‖u‖^2 + ‖v‖^2, then u and v are orthogonal.

items.label: 1i
items.text: The orthogonal projection of y onto u is a scalar multiple of y.

items.label: 1j
items.text: If a vector y coincides with its orthogonal projection onto a subspace W, then y is in W.

items.label: 1k
items.text: The set of all vectors in R^n orthogonal to one fixed vector is a subspace of R^n.

items.label: 1l
items.text: If W is a subspace of R^n, then W and W⊥ have no vectors in common.

items.label: 1m
items.text: If {v1, v2, v3} is an orthogonal set and if c1, c2, and c3 are scalars, then {c1 v1, c2 v2, c3 v3} is an orthogonal set.

items.label: 1n
items.text: If a matrix U has orthonormal columns, then UU^T = I.

items.label: 1o
items.text: A square matrix with orthogonal columns is an orthogonal matrix.

items.label: 1p
items.text: If a square matrix has orthonormal columns, then it also has orthonormal rows.

items.label: 1q
items.text: If W is a subspace, then ‖proj_W v‖^2 + ‖v − proj_W v‖^2 = ‖v‖^2.

items.label: 1r
items.text: A least-squares solution of Ax = b is the vector A x̂ in Col A closest to b, so that ‖b − A x̂‖ ≤ ‖b − Ax‖ for all x.

items.label: 1s
items.text: The normal equations for a least-squares solution of Ax = b are given by x̂ = (A^T A)^-1 A^T b.
